Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
This matter comes on calendar having been set this date, February 25, 2025, for Plaintiffs' Application for Default Judgment Against Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc. / Prove-up Hearing.

Dept. 304, Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Proceedings Recorded by For The Record. Bailiff: Not Present.

Kimberly Chu, Esq., of Brayton Purcell, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiffs MARGO WRIGHT, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of RONALD WRIGHT, Deceased; and DENNIS WRIGHT and RONALD WRIGHT, JR., as Wrongful Death Heirs of RONALD WRIGHT, Deceased.

No appearance by defaulted defendants KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC., and K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.

Order waiving personal appearance of all witnesses at the Default Prove-up Hearing was filed on January 22, 2025.

Default of defendants has been duly entered.

The Court states that it has reviewed all documents in support of the default and admits all exhibits.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendants KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC., and K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. in the amount of:

As to KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.,
$2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
$658,882.06 in Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $2,658,882.06

As to K-M INDURSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.,
$2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
$658,882.06 in Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $2,658,882.06

Judgment signed as modified in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF MARGO WRIGHT's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint:

APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Taylor N. Donoho of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (619.230.7453), appearing for Defendant, Hennessy Industries, LLC.
Celeste Scott of Prindle Goetz Barnes & Reinholtz, LLP (415.788.8354), appearing for Defendant, Chevron USA, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted.
Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. Plaintiff provided the Court with a copy of the amended pleading, which is serially numbered to differentiate it from previous pleadings, and states what allegations regarding wrongful death are proposed to be added to the previous pleading in the personal injury action and where the additional allegations are located. (CRC 3.1324(a).) Plaintiff submitted a declaration specifying the effect of the amendment, why the amendment is necessary and proper, when the facts giving rise to the amended allegations were discovered, and the reasons why the request was not made earlier. (CRC 3.1324(b).) As of the date of the granting of Plaintiff's motion, the Second Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared under C.C.P. section 128(a)(3).

Order is signed.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Not reported. (503/CML)
